title: Fresh rebuild: Top 100 crypto tracker with filters and 12-month price projections

requirements.summary: Replace all existing dashboard components with a single clean Top100Dashboard component that fetches and displays the top 100 cryptocurrencies from CoinGecko in a paginated, sortable table with 8 required columns including Buy Ratio
acceptanceCriteria: Table renders exactly 100 rows of live CoinGecko data on load
All 8 columns are present and correctly populated
Buy Ratio column shows (volume/marketCap)*100 formatted as a percentage
Table is sortable by clicking any column header
Table is paginated (e.g. 25 rows per page) with page navigation controls
Coin logo thumbnails are displayed alongside name and symbol
All previous dashboard components (Top150Dashboard, MultiCryptoDashboard, etc.) are removed from the rendered page

file_operations.description: Create the main Top100Dashboard component that fetches top 100 coins from CoinGecko (/api/v3/coins/markets?vs_currency=usd&order=market_cap_desc&per_page=100&page=1&sparkline=false) using the useTopCoins hook. Display a paginated, sortable table with columns: Rank, Name/Symbol (with logo thumbnail), Price (USD), 24h Change (%), Market Cap, 24h Volume, Buy Ratio (calculated as (volume/marketCap)*100), and Fully Diluted Market Cap. Implement column-click sorting, pagination controls (25 rows per page), loading states, and error handling. Use Tailwind styling consistent with the dark-mode glassmorphism theme. Format positive/negative 24h changes in emerald/red respectively.

Replace the current Top150Dashboard render with the new Top100Dashboard component. Remove all references to legacy dashboards (Top150Dashboard, MultiCryptoDashboard, ICPDashboard, Top50Dashboard, etc.). Keep QueryClient setup, ThemeProvider, Header, and Footer intact. Ensure the App renders only Header, Top100Dashboard, and Footer in that order.

requirements.summary: Add a comprehensive filter and sort control bar above the Top 100 table with text search, four metric-specific dropdown sorters, and a Reset button
acceptanceCriteria: Search input filters the table rows in real time by coin name or ticker symbol (case-insensitive)
Each of the four metric dropdowns correctly reorders the visible rows when a sort direction is selected
Only one sort criterion is active at a time; selecting a new dropdown deselects the previous one
Reset button restores the default sort order and clears the search field
Filter bar is fully visible and usable at 375 px mobile width

file_operations.description: Create a filter bar component with: a text search input (filters by coin name/symbol, case-insensitive); four dropdown selectors for sorting by Live Market Cap, 24h Volume, Buy Ratio, and Fully Diluted Market Cap (each with 'High→Low' and 'Low→High' options); and a Reset button that clears all filters and returns to default market-cap-desc order. Ensure only one sort criterion is active at a time. Use controlled state with onChange handlers for each control. Apply glassmorphism card styling and ensure full responsiveness down to 375px mobile width. Emit filter/sort state to parent via callback props.

Integrate the Top100FilterBar component above the table. Wire filter/sort state from the filter bar into the table rendering logic. Apply search filtering and metric-based sorting to the coin data before pagination. Ensure the Reset button callback clears all active filters and restores the default order.

requirements.summary: Add a dedicated Price Projections section below the Top 100 table with a searchable coin selector, 12-month projection table, and Recharts line chart, all computed client-side using historical data and projections.ts logic
acceptanceCriteria: Coin selector dropdown is searchable and lists all 100 coins
Selecting a coin triggers a CoinGecko 30-day history fetch and displays the projection panel
Projection table shows 12 rows (one per month) with low, mid, high prices and a confidence score
Confidence score decreases gradually for later months to reflect growing uncertainty
Line chart renders mid-price trajectory across 12 months with month labels on the x-axis
Loading and error states are handled gracefully with user-facing messages
No external AI API calls are made; all projection logic is algorithmic and runs in the browser

file_operations.description: Update the existing PriceProjectionsSection component to accept a list of the top 100 coins as a prop (passed from Top100Dashboard). Replace any hardcoded coin list with the dynamic prop. Ensure the searchable coin selector dropdown populates from this list. When a coin is selected, fetch its 30-day price history using the useCoinHistory hook, compute 12-month projections using frontend/src/utils/projections.ts, and display a monthly table (Month 1–12) with low/mid/high prices and confidence scores. Render a Recharts LineChart or AreaChart visualizing the mid-price trajectory across all 12 months with month labels on the x-axis. Handle loading, error, and empty states with user-facing messages. Ensure no external AI API calls are made.
Render the PriceProjectionsSection component below the Top 100 table. Pass the fetched top 100 coins array as a prop to PriceProjectionsSection so it can populate its coin selector dropdown.

Review and adapt the existing projection logic to ensure it computes 12-month low/mid/high price projections and confidence scores from 30-day historical price data using linear regression and volatility calculations. Ensure confidence scores decrease gradually for later months. Verify compatibility with the data structure returned by useCoinHistory. No changes needed if already compatible; otherwise, adjust input/output types as required.

requirements.summary: Implement a clean React Query data-fetching layer with useTopCoins (60s cache) and useCoinHistory (5m cache) hooks, including rate-limit-safe retry logic and fallback to cached data on errors
acceptanceCriteria: useTopCoins returns { data, isLoading, isError } and caches results for 60 seconds
useCoinHistory accepts a coin ID string and returns cached 30-day price arrays
On API error, both hooks return the last successful cached value if available
Retry logic uses exponential backoff and does not exceed 3 attempts
No duplicate API calls fire when multiple components consume the same hook simultaneously

file_operations.description: Replace the existing useTopCoins hook to fetch exactly 100 coins (not 150) from CoinGecko /api/v3/coins/markets with params: vs_currency=usd, order=market_cap_desc, per_page=100, page=1, sparkline=false. Set staleTime to 60 seconds (60000ms) and enable in-memory caching. Ensure the useCoinHistory hook fetches 30-day price history for a given coin ID from /api/v3/coins/{id}/market_chart?vs_currency=usd&days=30 with a 5-minute staleTime (300000ms). Implement rate-limit-safe retry logic with exponential backoff (max 3 retries) for both hooks. Configure React Query to fall back to the last successful cached value on API errors (gcTime and retry settings). Ensure no duplicate API calls when multiple components use the same hook.

requirements.summary: Apply a cohesive dark-mode-first visual theme across the entire rebuilt application with near-black base, neon cyan primary accent, gold secondary accent, sticky branded header, glassmorphism styling, and full mobile responsiveness down to 375px
acceptanceCriteria: Background is near-black charcoal; neon cyan and gold are used for accents and highlights
Header is sticky and displays 'Crypto Tracker AI' with a pulsing live badge
Table rows, filter cards, and dropdowns have a frosted/glassmorphism appearance
Positive percentage changes are rendered in emerald green, negative in red
Layout renders correctly and is usable at 375 px viewport width with no horizontal scroll
Dark mode is the default; no light-mode toggle is required

file_operations.description: Update the global CSS to enforce a near-black charcoal base background (#0d0f14 or equivalent) as the default. Ensure neon cyan is the primary accent color and gold is the secondary accent. Verify that glassmorphism utilities (frosted panels, subtle backdrop blur) are defined and applied. Confirm that positive/negative price change classes use emerald green and red respectively. Ensure all base styles support mobile down to 375px viewport width with no horizontal scroll.

Update the Tailwind config to include near-black charcoal (#0d0f14 or equivalent) as the default background color, neon cyan as the primary accent, and gold as the secondary accent in the color palette. Ensure custom utilities for glassmorphism (backdrop-blur, frosted panels) are defined. Verify responsive breakpoints support 375px mobile width. Confirm emerald and red colors are mapped for positive/negative indicators.

Update the Header component to be sticky (position: sticky, top: 0, z-index high). Ensure it displays 'Crypto Tracker AI' branding prominently with a pulsing live indicator badge (e.g., a small animated dot or pill). Remove or hide the theme toggle button if present, since dark mode is the default and required. Apply glassmorphism styling (frosted background, subtle border) and ensure full responsiveness at 375px mobile width.

Apply glassmorphism styling to table rows, header, and pagination controls. Ensure positive 24h percentage changes render in emerald green and negative in red. Verify the table layout is fully responsive at 375px mobile width with no horizontal scroll (use responsive tables or card-based mobile layout if needed). Use Tailwind classes consistent with the updated color palette (near-black base, neon cyan primary, gold secondary).

Apply glassmorphism card styling to the filter bar (frosted background, subtle border, backdrop blur). Use neon cyan for active filter highlights and gold for secondary accents. Ensure all inputs and dropdowns are fully visible and usable at 375px mobile width. Use responsive flexbox or grid layout to stack controls vertically on narrow screens if needed.

Apply glassmorphism styling to the projection panel, selector dropdown, and chart container. Use neon cyan for primary highlights and gold for secondary accents. Ensure the layout is fully responsive at 375px mobile width. Use Tailwind classes consistent with the updated color palette (near-black base, emerald/red for projections if applicable).
